question: What type of game is Cosmic Signature?

options.label: A

question: What is the name of the main game that runs the bidding rounds?
What kind of token is CST?

options.label: C

question: What kind of token is the Cosmic Signature NFT?

options.label: D
options.text: Any type of bid is acceptable as the first bid

options.label: A

options.label: B
options.text: Safekeeping secondary prizes, donated tokens, and donated NFTs until winners withdraw them

question: How many stages does each bidding round go through?

question: Who has the ability to create new CST tokens?

options.label: C
options.text: Only the Cosmic Signature Game itself (automatically, as part of game mechanics)

question: Can the Cosmic Signature Game be upgraded after deployment?

options.label: A

question: Since the time added per bid grows slightly each round, what effect does this have on the Dutch auction durations?

options.label: A
options.text: Dutch auctions get shorter each round

options.label: B
options.text: Dutch auctions get slightly longer each round (because they are derived from the same time increment value)

options.label: C
options.text: Dutch auctions stay exactly the same length

options.label: D
options.text: Only the ETH Dutch auction gets longer; the CST auction stays the same

question: The initial time until the main prize can be claimed (after the first bid) is approximately 1 day. If Alice places the first bid at noon on Monday, approximately when could the main prize first be claimed if no other bids are placed?

Each bid after the first extends the deadline. If the deadline is currently set for Tuesday at noon and the time increment is 1 hour, a bid placed on Monday evening would set the new deadline to approximately when?

options.label: B
options.text: Tuesday at noon + 1 hour = Tuesday at 1:00 PM (because Tuesday noon is later than Monday evening)

question: What if the deadline has already passed (say it was Tuesday at noon) and a bid is placed on Wednesday at 3:00 PM with a 1-hour time increment? What is the new deadline?

options.label: A
options.text: Tuesday at noon + 1 hour = Tuesday at 1:00 PM

options.label: B
options.text: Wednesday at 3:00 PM + 1 hour = Wednesday at 4:00 PM (the game uses the later of the current deadline or the current time)

question: Who is allowed to claim the main prize, and when?

options.label: A
options.text: Only the last bidder, at any time after their bid

options.label: B
options.text: The last bidder once the deadline passes; if they don't claim within an additional 1-day window, anyone can claim it

options.label: C
options.text: Any bidder once the deadline passes

question: If the last bidder does not claim the main prize within the 1-day timeout, and a different person claims it, what happens?

options.label: A
options.text: The prizes go to the original last bidder anyway

options.label: B
options.text: The new claimant receives the main ETH prize, a CS NFT, and a CST bonus -- they become the "beneficiary" of this round

options.label: C
options.text: The new claimant only receives the ETH

question: Why is the Chrono-Warrior title also evaluated right at the moment the main prize is claimed?

options.label: B
options.text: To give the current Endurance Champion one final chance to also be recognized as Chrono-Warrior, accounting for their streak right up to the end of the round

options.label: C
options.text: To calculate the next round's chrono starting point

options.label: D
options.text: It is just a redundant safety check

question: Scenario: Round ends with 10 ETH. Main prize (25%) = 2.5 ETH to the winner. The Chrono-Warrior ETH prize (8%) = 0.8 ETH. But the Chrono-Warrior's ETH prize is NOT sent directly to them -- it goes to the Prizes Wallet instead. Why?

options.label: B
options.text: So the Chrono-Warrior can withdraw it when convenient, with the same timeout protection as other secondary prizes

options.label: C
options.text: Because the Chrono-Warrior might reject the ETH

options.label: D
options.text: The DAO must approve the withdrawal first

question: The game owner has a special ability to reduce the ETH price floor if nobody is willing to bid. What conditions must be met for the owner to use this ability?

options.label: A
options.text: The owner can use it at any time

options.label: B
options.text: No bids have been placed yet in the current round, and the Dutch auction period has already ended

options.label: D
options.text: A DAO vote must approve it

question: When the owner uses the price-floor reduction ability, what happens to the current ETH bid price?

options.label: A
options.text: It is cut roughly in half, and the auction duration is adjusted so the price doesn't jump suddenly

options.label: B
options.text: It is reset to the starting price

question: Scenario: Alice wins 0.5 ETH in the round 5 raffle and 0.3 ETH in the round 7 raffle. Can she withdraw both prizes in a single transaction?

options.label: A
options.text: No, she must withdraw each round separately

options.label: B
options.text: Yes, both balances are combined (0.8 ETH total) and sent to her in one transfer

options.label: C
options.text: She can only withdraw from the most recent round

question: A player donated an NFT to the prize pool during round 5. Who can claim this donated NFT, and when?

options.label: B
options.text: The main prize winner of round 5 can claim it immediately; after the withdrawal timeout, anyone can claim it

question: A player donated tokens (like USDC or another crypto token) to the prize pool during round 5. After the withdrawal timeout expires, who can claim the remaining tokens?

options.label: A
options.text: Only the main prize winner of round 5

options.label: B
options.text: Anyone -- the timeout has passed and the tokens are open for anyone to claim

question: When someone other than a prize winner withdraws ETH from the Prizes Wallet after the timeout, where does the ETH actually go?

options.label: A
options.text: To the original prize winner's wallet

options.label: B
options.text: To the wallet of whoever made the withdrawal

question: Timeline: Alice bids at time 0 (first bid). Bob bids at time 50. Charlie bids at time 200. Dave bids at time 300. What are the last-bidder durations?
In Q144, who is the Endurance Champion when Dave bids?

options.label: B
options.text: Bob (150s -- the longest single continuous stretch)

question: In Q144-145, when Dave bids at time 300, Charlie had been last bidder for 100 seconds. Since Charlie's 100 seconds does not exceed Bob's record of 150 seconds, what happens?

options.label: A
options.text: Charlie becomes the new Endurance Champion

options.label: B
options.text: Dave becomes the new Endurance Champion

options.label: C
options.text: Bob remains the Endurance Champion because Charlie's streak was shorter

question: When does the Chrono-Warrior title actually get evaluated during a round?

options.label: B
options.text: Only when a new Endurance Champion is crowned (someone beats the current record), and once more when the round ends

question: Scenario: 5 Cosmic Signature NFTs are staked. A reward deposit of 10 ETH arrives. Each NFT gets 2 ETH of reward. Player A staked early (when cumulative reward per NFT was 1.0). Player B staked recently (when cumulative reward per NFT was 3.0). After the deposit, cumulative reward is 4.0. What does each earn if they unstake?
In Q148, Player A earns 3.0 ETH and Player B earns 1.0 ETH. Their total is 4.0 ETH. But 10 ETH was deposited and 5 NFTs are staked. Where is the other 6.0 ETH?

options.label: B
options.text: It belongs to the other 3 stakers -- they will collect it when they unstake

question: Consider this staking scenario: (1) Alice stakes when cumulative reward is 0. (2) Bob stakes when cumulative reward is 0. (3) 1 ETH deposited with 2 NFTs staked -- each gets 0.5, cumulative goes to 0.5. (4) Carol stakes when cumulative reward is 0.5. (5) 0.9 ETH deposited with 3 NFTs staked -- each gets 0.3, cumulative goes to 0.8. If all three unstake, what are their rewards?
In Q150, total rewards = 0.8 + 0.8 + 0.3 = 1.9 ETH. Total deposited = 1.0 + 0.9 = 1.9 ETH. It balances perfectly in this case. Would it always balance perfectly?

options.label: B
options.text: Usually, but extremely small rounding errors can occur in edge cases because whole-number division doesn't always divide evenly

options.label: C
options.text: No, there would always be significant losses

options.label: D
options.text: Only if there's an even number of stakers

question: The prize distribution percentages add up to 50% (25% main + 8% chrono + 4% raffle + 6% stakers + 7% charity). Does exactly 50% remain in the game for the next round?

options.label: B
options.text: Approximately 50%, but small rounding differences may cause slight deviations

options.label: C
options.text: No, the percentages are applied one after another so significantly less than 50% remains

options.label: D
options.text: Yes, the math is always perfectly precise

question: If the game's prize pool has been growing over many rounds (because ~50% stays each time), what effect does this have on future prizes?

options.label: A
options.text: Future prizes stay the same size because the percentages are fixed

options.label: B
options.text: Future prizes grow larger in absolute ETH terms because the pool is bigger, even though the percentages are the same

options.label: C
options.text: Future prizes shrink because ETH is being continuously removed

options.label: D
options.text: Future prizes are exactly the same as in round 1

question: Scenario: A round has 50 ETH and 100 bids were placed. Alice is the last bidder and claims the main prize. Walk through the ETH distribution with default percentages. How much approximately remains for the next round?

options.label: B
options.text: 25 ETH (50% of the total pool remains)

question: In Q154, the main prize is 25% of 50 = 12.5 ETH. But where does Alice's 12.5 ETH actually come from?

options.label: B
options.text: Directly from the game's ETH balance, sent to Alice immediately during the claim

options.label: D
options.text: It is minted as new ETH

question: After the Chrono-Warrior ETH prize is deposited into the Prizes Wallet, the Chrono-Warrior must take action to receive it. What must they do?

options.label: B
options.text: They must withdraw it from the Prizes Wallet before the timeout expires

options.label: C
options.text: They must stake a Cosmic Signature NFT first

options.label: D
options.text: They must wait for the next round to begin

question: Scenario: The ETH Dutch auction starts at 0.0004 ETH and drops to a floor of about 0.000002 ETH over 2 hours. After 1 hour (exactly halfway), what is the approximate ETH bid price?

options.label: A
options.text: About 0.0002 ETH (halfway between start and floor)

question: Why does the ETH Dutch auction have a floor price greater than zero, while the CST Dutch auction drops all the way to zero?

options.label: A
options.text: There is no reason -- both reach zero

options.label: B
options.text: The ETH auction has a minimum floor to ensure bidders always pay some ETH, while the CST auction is designed to eventually allow free CST bids

options.label: C
options.text: The CST auction has the floor, not the ETH auction

options.label: D
options.text: Both auctions have floors above zero

question: If a round's ETH Dutch auction ends and nobody has bid yet, the price sits at the floor. The game owner can halve this floor. What practical effect does this have for players?

options.label: A
options.text: It doubles the cost of bidding

options.label: B
options.text: It makes bidding cheaper by lowering the minimum ETH price, encouraging someone to place the first bid

options.label: C
options.text: It has no effect on players

question: Timeline: Alice bids at time 1000 (first bid). Bob bids at time 1050. Alice bids again at time 1100. Carol bids at time 1400. At each point, who is the last bidder and for how long?

options.label: B
options.text: Alice: 50s, Bob: 50s, Alice (again): 200s, Carol takes over

question: In Q160, multiple consecutive bids from the same person (Alice bidding at 1000 and again at 1100) -- does this extend their last-bidder streak, or does it reset?

options.label: A
options.text: Each of Alice's bids resets her streak timer to the time of the new bid

options.label: B
options.text: Alice's consecutive bids accumulate -- her streak continues from the original bid

options.label: C
options.text: Only the first bid from a player counts

question: In Q160-161, Alice bids at 1000, Bob bids at 1050 (taking over), Alice bids at 1100 (taking back). Alice's new streak starts at 1100, not 1000. So when Carol bids at 1400, Alice's current streak is 300 seconds (1100 to 1400). Is this correct?

options.label: A
options.text: Yes -- Alice's current streak is from her most recent bid (1100) to when she was outbid (1400) = 300 seconds

options.label: B
options.text: No -- Alice's streak is from 1000 to 1400 = 400 seconds

options.label: C
options.text: No -- Alice's streak is only 50 seconds

options.label: D
options.text: Alice has no streak because she bid twice

question: Why is the "initial duration until main prize" set to approximately 1 day for the first round?

options.label: A
options.text: To give many players time to join the round before the deadline approaches

options.label: C
options.text: It's an arbitrary choice with no strategic reason

options.label: D
options.text: To prevent anyone from winning too quickly in the very first round only

question: If a player places a CST bid when no ETH bids have been placed yet in the round, what happens?

options.label: A
options.text: The CST bid goes through normally

options.label: B
options.text: The bid is rejected -- the first bid in every round must be ETH

options.label: C
options.text: The CST bid is converted to an ETH bid

options.label: D
options.text: The player is refunded their CST

question: A scenario: The game has been running for 100 rounds. Each round, the time increment grows by ~1%. In round 100, the time increment is noticeably larger than round 1. What practical effect does this have on gameplay?

options.label: A
options.text: Rounds get shorter and more intense

options.label: B
options.text: Rounds get longer because each bid pushes the deadline further into the future, and the Dutch auctions also last longer

options.label: D
options.text: The game becomes cheaper to play

question: Players can attach messages to their bids. What is the maximum message length?
If a player owns a Random Walk NFT that has already been used for a discounted bid in a previous round, can they use it again?

options.label: B
options.text: No -- once a Random Walk NFT is used for a discounted bid, it can never be used for bidding again

options.label: C
options.text: Yes, but at a smaller discount

options.label: D
options.text: Only if they stake it first

question: If a player owns a Random Walk NFT that has already been staked once and then unstaked, can they stake it again?

options.label: B
options.text: No -- once an NFT has been staked, it cannot be staked again even after unstaking

options.label: D
options.text: Only after waiting one full round

question: A player staked their Random Walk NFT. Now they want to use it for a discounted ETH bid. Can they?

options.label: A
options.text: Yes, staking and bidding uses are separate

options.label: B
options.text: No, the NFT is locked in the staking wallet and must be unstaked first before it could be used for bidding (though each use is tracked separately)

options.label: C
options.text: Yes, the staking wallet allows bidding directly

options.label: D
options.text: No, because staked NFTs are consumed

question: The Cosmic Signature DAO allows CST holders to vote on proposals. What happens during the 2-day voting delay period?

options.label: B
options.text: The proposal has been submitted but voting has not yet started -- this gives people time to review the proposal

question: After the 2-day delay, the DAO voting period opens for 2 weeks. What percentage of CST must participate for the vote to be valid?
Who can create a proposal in the Cosmic Signature DAO?

options.label: C
options.text: Anyone who holds at least a minimum threshold of CST (set to the same amount as the bidding reward)

question: Scenario: Alice has been the last bidder since time 5000. Bob bids at time 5500. Alice was last bidder for 500 seconds. The current Endurance Champion record is 300 seconds (held by Carol). What happens?

options.label: B
options.text: Alice becomes the new Endurance Champion because 500 > 300, and Carol's chrono segment is evaluated for the Chrono-Warrior title

options.label: D
options.text: Both Alice and Carol share the title

question: In Q173, when Alice dethroned Carol as Endurance Champion, Carol's "chrono segment" is evaluated. If Carol's chrono duration was 250 seconds and the current Chrono-Warrior's duration is 200 seconds, what happens?

options.label: A
options.text: Carol becomes the new Chrono-Warrior with 250 seconds

options.label: B
options.text: The existing Chrono-Warrior keeps the title

options.label: C
options.text: Carol and the current CW share the title

question: At round end, the game does one final evaluation of the current Endurance Champion's chrono segment. Why is this important?

options.label: A
options.text: It's not important -- just a formality

options.label: B
options.text: Because the current EC may have held their title through the very end of the round, and that final chrono duration might be the longest, making the current EC also the Chrono-Warrior

options.label: C
options.text: To reset the champion for the next round

question: Scenario: 20 ETH in the pool. The main prize (25%) is calculated FIRST but actually SENT LAST. The Chrono-Warrior prize (8%) is calculated and deposited before the main prize is sent. Does the main prize calculation use the 20 ETH or some reduced amount?

options.label: A
options.text: A reduced amount -- after the chrono prize is deposited

options.label: B
options.text: The full 20 ETH -- all percentage calculations are done before any ETH leaves the game

options.label: C
options.text: It depends on the order of operations

question: A Cosmic Signature NFT is given a custom name "StarWalker" by its owner. If the NFT is transferred to a new owner, can the new owner change the name?

options.label: A
options.text: No, only the original namer can change it

options.label: B
options.text: Yes, the new owner (or anyone they authorize) can change the name

options.label: C
options.text: Only the game can change NFT names

options.label: D
options.text: NFT names cannot be changed once set

question: Each Cosmic Signature NFT has a unique seed that determines how it looks visually. How is this seed generated?

options.label: B
options.text: It is derived from on-chain random data at the time of minting

options.label: C
options.text: It is always the same as the NFT's ID number

options.label: D
options.text: It is provided by an off-chain service

question: Scenario: The CST Dutch auction starts at 200 CST. A player bids and pays 100 CST. What is the new starting price for the next CST auction within the same round?

options.label: B
options.text: 200 CST (the minimum limit kicks in, since 100 x 2 = 200 equals the minimum)

question: In Q179, if the player had paid 150 CST instead of 100, what would the new starting price be?

options.label: B
options.text: 300 CST (150 x 2 = 300, which exceeds the 200 minimum, so 300 is used)

question: A round has accumulated donations: some crypto tokens and three NFTs. The main prize winner claims the main prize. Can they immediately claim the donated items too?

options.label: A
options.text: No, they must wait for a separate claim period

options.label: B
options.text: Yes, the main prize winner has immediate access to claim donated tokens and NFTs from the Prizes Wallet

options.label: C
options.text: Donated items are automatically sent to the winner

options.label: D
options.text: Donated items go to random bidders

question: What is the order of prize distribution at round end?

options.label: A
options.text: Main prize first, then everything else

options.label: B
options.text: Secondary prizes (raffle ETH, chrono ETH, staker ETH, charity) are deposited/sent first, then the main ETH prize is sent directly to the claimant last

question: The game tracks how much ETH and CST each bidder spent per round. Why might this information be useful?

options.label: B
options.text: It could be used for future features, analytics, or to determine eligibility for special rewards

options.label: C
options.text: It is used to calculate the main prize amount

options.label: D
options.text: It determines the Dutch auction starting price

question: A new round begins after the 30-minute delay. The ETH Dutch auction starts dropping from a high price. But the first CST bid can only happen after what?

options.label: B
options.text: After at least one ETH bid has been placed (because the first bid in every round must be ETH)

options.label: C
options.text: After 1 hour of the round being active

options.label: D
options.text: CST bids can be placed at any time

question: In how many different ways can a player place an ETH bid?

options.label: B
options.text: Multiple ways: plain ETH bid, ETH bid with Random Walk NFT discount, ETH bid with token donation, ETH bid with NFT donation, or simply sending ETH to the game address

options.label: C
options.text: Two ways: with or without a message

options.label: D
options.text: Three ways: small, medium, and large bids

question: Scenario: A player places an ETH bid and simultaneously donates an NFT they own to the prize pool. What happens to the donated NFT?

options.label: B
options.text: It is transferred to the Prizes Wallet and can be claimed by the round's main prize winner (or anyone after timeout)

options.label: C
options.text: It stays in the player's wallet

options.label: D
options.text: It goes directly to the last bidder

question: What is the approximate percentage of the ETH pool that stays in the game after prize distribution? (Add up: 25 + 8 + 4 + 6 + 7 = 50% distributed.)
If the game has been running for 50 rounds and approximately 50% of ETH stays each round, won't the pool eventually become huge?

options.label: B
options.text: Yes, the pool grows as new ETH from bids continuously enters while only ~50% leaves each round -- this creates growing prize pools over time

options.label: D
options.text: The pool actually shrinks each round

question: How are the raffle prize winners for ETH selected differently than the raffle winners for Cosmic Signature NFTs?

options.label: B
options.text: They are selected using the same random mechanism -- random draws from all bids in the round -- but they are separate draws, so a player could win both an ETH raffle and an NFT raffle

question: Can a bidder who placed only one bid in a round with 100 total bids still win a raffle prize?

options.label: A
options.text: No, you need at least 10 bids to qualify

options.label: B
options.text: Yes -- even a single bid gives them a 1-in-100 chance per raffle draw

options.label: C
options.text: No, only the top bidders qualify

options.label: D
options.text: Only if they used a Random Walk NFT

question: Scenario: Alice placed 40 out of 100 bids in a round. There are 3 ETH raffle draws. What is the approximate probability that Alice wins at least one ETH raffle prize?

options.label: B
options.text: About 78% (1 minus the probability of losing all 3 draws: 1 - (60/100)^3)

question: The game supports "ETH donations with info" -- players can donate ETH and attach a message. How is this different from a regular bid?

options.label: B
options.text: Donations add ETH to the pool and record a message, but do NOT count as a bid -- the donor does not become the last bidder and the timer is not extended

options.label: C
options.text: Donations replace the current bid price

question: What happens to the CST Dutch auction starting price for the NEXT round?

options.label: A
options.text: It is reset to a fixed default

options.label: B
options.text: It is carried forward from the first CST bid of the current round -- the price that was set when the first CST bid was placed becomes the starting point for the next round's first CST auction

options.label: D
options.text: It doubles from the current round

question: Scenario: In round 5, the first ETH bid price is 0.001 ETH. The next round's starting Dutch auction price will be 0.002 ETH (double). If the first bid in round 6 goes through at 0.0015 ETH (after the price drops from 0.002), what does the round 7 starting price become?

options.label: B
options.text: 0.003 ETH (double of the first bid price of round 6)

question: After 3 consecutive ETH bids in a round (excluding the first which follows the Dutch auction), each bid increases the price by about 1%. If the first bid set the price at 0.001 ETH, approximately what is the price after the third consecutive bid?

The Endurance Champion is the player with the longest single continuous last-bidder streak. But what if nobody outbids the first bidder for the entire round? Is the first (and only) bidder the Endurance Champion?

options.label: A
options.text: No -- you need at least two bidders for the Endurance Champion title to be assigned

options.label: B
options.text: Yes -- the first bidder held the last-bidder position for the entire round, making them the Endurance Champion

options.label: C
options.text: The title is not assigned if there is only one bidder

options.label: D
options.text: The game requires at least 10 bids for champion titles

question: Can the Endurance Champion and the Chrono-Warrior be the same person in the same round?

options.label: A
options.text: No, they must always be different players

options.label: B
options.text: Yes -- if the same player held the longest single last-bidder streak AND held the Endurance Champion record for the longest period, they could earn both titles

options.label: D
options.text: Only if there are fewer than 3 bidders

question: A player staked a Cosmic Signature NFT and has earned 2 ETH in rewards. They want to unstake. What happens?

options.label: A
options.text: They get their NFT back but lose the ETH reward

options.label: B
options.text: They get their NFT back AND receive their accumulated ETH reward in the same transaction

options.label: C
options.text: They must claim the reward in a separate transaction

options.label: D
options.text: The reward is converted to CST

question: Can a player stake multiple Cosmic Signature NFTs at the same time?

options.label: A
options.text: No, only one NFT per wallet

options.label: B
options.text: Yes, and each staked NFT earns rewards independently based on when it was staked

options.label: C
options.text: Yes, but all NFTs in a wallet earn the same amount regardless of when they were staked

question:
Final comprehensive scenario: A round has 40 ETH in the pool. 200 bids were placed by 50 different players. Alice is the last bidder. Bob is the Endurance Champion. Carol is the Chrono-Warrior. Dave placed the last CST bid. The round ends and Alice claims the main prize. Walk through the prize distribution:

Main Prize (25%): Alice gets 10 ETH + 1 CS NFT + 1,000 CST

Last CST Bidder: Dave gets 1 CS NFT + 1,000 CST

Endurance Champion: Bob gets 1 CS NFT + 1,000 CST

Chrono-Warrior (8% ETH): Carol gets 3.2 ETH (deposited to Prizes Wallet) + 1 CS NFT + 1,000 CST

Bidder ETH Raffle (4% split 3 ways): ~0.533 ETH each to 3 random bidders

Bidder NFT Raffle: 10 random bidders each get 1 CS NFT + 1,000 CST

RW NFT Staker Raffle: 10 random stakers each get 1 CS NFT + 1,000 CST (if any are staked)

CS NFT Stakers (6%): 2.4 ETH distributed among all CS NFT stakers

Charity (7%): 2.8 ETH to charity

Marketing: 3,000 CST to Marketing Wallet

Approximately how much ETH remains in the game for round 2?
